title: Valentina Allegra de Fontaine
content:
{{Character |image = Valentina Allegra de Fontaine Infobox.png |real name = Valentina Allegra de Fontaine{{Ref|TFaTWS105}} |alias = {{Alias |derivatives = Val{{Ref|TFaTWS105}}}} |species = [[Human]] |citizenship = {{Citizenship|ITLY}}
{{Citizenship|USA}} |DOB = [[1960s]]-[[1980s]]{{Ref|TB}} |gender = Female |title = Contessa
{{WPS|Director of the Central Intelligence Agency|Director}} of the [[CIA]] |affiliation = {{Affiliation|OXE}}
{{Affiliation|CIA}} |movie = ''[[Black Widow (film)|Black Widow]]'' ([[List of Post-credits Scenes|post-credits scene]])
''[[Black Panther: Wakanda Forever]]''
''[[Thunderbolts*]]'' |tv series = ''[[The Falcon and The Winter Soldier]]'' (2 episodes) |comic = ''[[Thunderbolts* Dossier]]'' |actor = [[Julia Louis-Dreyfus]]
[[Chiara Stella]] (young) |status = Alive}} {{Quote|Mel, righteousness without power is just an opinion. Look, you're brought up to believe that there's a good guy and a bad guy, but then eventually you come to realize that there's a bad guy and a worse guy.|Valentina Allegra de Fontaine to [[Mel Gold]]|Thunderbolts*}} Contessa '''Valentina Allegra de Fontaine''' is a noblewoman, chairwoman of [[O.X.E.]] and the Director of the [[CIA]]. Interested in privatizing global security and gaining power within the United States government, de Fontaine sought out to recruit or create powerful individuals to serve as her enforcers. De Fontaine personally recruited [[John Walker]], who had lost his position as Captain America, and [[Yelena Belova]], who was feeling lost after losing her [[Natasha Romanoff|sister]], among others. When political tensions arose between the [[United States]] and [[Wakanda]], with the U.S. wishing to obtain [[vibranium]] for themselves, de Fontaine assigned her ex-husband and subordinate [[Everett Ross]], who strongly opposed any offensive actions against Wakanda, to work with her. However, de Fontaine knew Ross would betray them and [[Arrest of Everett Ross|arrested]] him for committing treason after she gained evidence of him giving classified intelligence to Wakanda. When facing [[Prosecution of Valentina Allegra de Fontaine|impeachment]] due to her connection to illegal black ops projects, de Fontaine ordered her enforcers to eliminate all traces of O.X.E., including [[Project Sentry]]. However, learning she sent them to kill each other, her agents turned against her and became the [[Thunderbolts]] after unintentionally reactivating [[Bob Reynolds]]. Seeing Reynolds as proof of Project Sentry’s success, de Fontaine attempted to control him as her personal weapon, but his dark alter ego, the Void, turned against her and threatened to engulf [[New York City]] in his darkness. After witnessing the Thunderbolts save civilians and desperate to protect her reputation, she publicly rebranded them as the New Avengers, although the team still had enough leverage on de Fontaine to keep her in check. ==Biography== {{MD|Valentina Allegra de Fontaine (Earth-199999)}} ===Early Life=== ====Murder of her Father==== {{Expand}} Valentina Allegra de Fontaine was an Italian Contessa.{{Ref|TFaTWS105}} When she was a little girl, she inadvertently led her father's [[Nico (Italy)|killer]] to him during the {{WPS|Years of Lead (Italy)|Years of Lead}} in [[Italy]], witnessing the murder, an event that would prompt her belief that there were only bad guys and worse guys in the world.{{Ref|TB}} ====Marriage to Everett Ross==== In her adulthood, de Fontaine moved to the [[United States]] and married [[Everett Ross]], although they eventually got divorced.{{Ref|BPWF}} De Fontaine founded [[O.X.E.]], a company that served as a front for various black ops project, which she needed to keep a secret.{{Ref|TB}} In [[2016]], in the aftermath of the [[Avengers Civil War]], de Fontaine purchased [[Avengers Tower]] from [[Tony Stark]], who moved all of its equipment to the [[Avengers Compound]].{{Ref|SMH}} ====[[Project Sentry]]==== While leading O.X.E., de Fontaine became interested in creating superpowered individuals to capitalize on the [[Avengers]]' absence, and launched [[Project Sentry]]. O.X.E. experimented on numerous test subjects under de Fontaine's orders to create a potential superpowered asset, one of them being [[Bob Reynolds]], although the project was deemed a failure after the majority of test subjects died. When de Fontaine was appointed as the Director of the [[CIA]], she publicly left a leading position at O.X.E. and served only as a consultant, while secretly retaining control over the company's operations.{{Ref|TB}} ===Finding Recruits=== ====Recruiting Taskmaster and Ghost==== Following the [[Blip]], de Fontaine began searching for special individuals to recruit to undertake shadow ops missions for [[O.X.E.]]{{Ref|MSCEUE}} De Fontaine recruited [[Antonia Dreykov]], a former [[Red Room]] operative, codenamed Taskmaster, with the ability to perfectly mimic the fighting techniques of other individuals. De Fontaine also recruited the former [[S.H.I.E.L.D.]] operative turned criminal [[Ava Starr]], also known as Ghost, who had the ability to become invisible and intangible. De Fontaine supplied both of her recruits with new suits and sent them on various missions, mainly assassinating high valued targets.{{Ref|TB}} ====Meeting with John Walker==== [[File:John Walker meets Valentina Allegra de Fontaine.png|{{AltFloat}}|De Fontaine first meeting with [[John Walker]]]] {{Quote|You did the right thing taking the [[Super Soldier Serum|serum]]. Yeah, of course I know about that. And I'll tell you something. It has made you very, very valuable to certain people.|Valentina Allegra de Fontaine to [[John Walker]]|Truth}} In [[2024]], following [[John Walker]] being stripped of his title as Captain America and military rank due to him murdering [[Nico]], a foreign national and member of the [[Flag Smashers]] who was surrendering, de Fontaine met with him and his wife, [[Olivia Walker]], at a bench outside the main courtroom in the [[United States Capitol]]. She introduced herself to the two, noting the name that she would prefer them to address her by, before she then awkwardly sat down between them. [[File:John, Valentina and Olivia.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|De Fontaine commenting on [[Nico]]'s death]] Sitting down, de Fontaine had told Walker that, if she had been in the same position that he had been in, she would have killed [[Nico]] too, as he had been responsible for the death of [[Battlestar]], while saying the only reason Walker was being punished was the government had to uphold their laws. She explained that she was aware Walker took the [[Super Soldier Serum]] before fighting the Flag Smashers, that he no longer possessed his [[Captain America's Shield|shield]], since [[Sam Wilson]] and [[Bucky Barnes]] had managed to subdue him and then reclaimed it, and that taking that serum made him valuable to certain people, whom de Fontaine was connected to. [[File:Valentina Allegra de Fontaine talks to John Walker.png|{{AltFloat}}|De Fontaine promising to call [[John Walker]]]] As Walker's wife tried to ask who de Fontaine was, she continued speaking to Walker and gave his wife a blank business card. She told Walker that his decision to take the serum was the best decision he had ever made, along with choosing to marry Olivia. She told Walker that the next best decision he would make would be to pick up the phone when she called him. She noted that the shield did not belong to the government before leaving.{{Ref|TFaTWS105}} ====Creating U.S. Agent==== [[File:Valentina looks at US Agent.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|De Fontaine admires [[John Walker]]'s [[U.S. Agent's Uniform|new suit]]]] {{Quote|Things are about to get weird. So when they do, we're not gonna need a Captain America. We're gonna need... a U.S. Agent.|Valentina Allegra de Fontaine to [[John Walker]]|One World, One People}} With the [[Flag Smashers]] defeated, de Fontaine reunited with [[John Walker]], after he had helped in the fight. While Walker put on his uniform, de Fontaine talked with [[Olivia Walker]] about all but one of the remaining Flag Smashers being killed while being transported to the [[Raft]], saying that [[Helmut Zemo]], who intended to destroy all super soldiers, had gotten the last laugh after all. Walker then stepped into the room in a [[U.S. Agent's Uniform|new version of his suit]], now all-black. As de Fontaine complimented the suit, she told the Walkers that the world was changing and that people did not need a [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]], giving Walker the new name of "[[U.S. Agent]]" before leaving.{{Ref|TFaTWS106}} ====Meeting with Yelena Belova==== [[File:Valentina Allegra de Fontaine & Yelena Belova.png|{{AltFloat}}|De Fontaine speaking with [[Yelena Belova]]]] {{Quote|Maybe you'd like a shot at the [[Hawkeye|man]] responsible for your [[Black Widow|sister]]'s [[Sacrifice of Natasha Romanoff|death]].|Valentina Allegra de Fontaine to [[Yelena Belova]]|Black Widow (film)}} Contessa de Fontaine met [[Yelena Belova]], a trained assassin and spy and the surrogate sister of [[Natasha Romanoff]].{{Ref|BW}} In [[2024|December 2024]], de Fontaine was contacted by [[Eleanor Bishop]] to assassinate [[Clint Barton]].{{Ref|HE105}} De Fontaine visited Romanoff's grave to speak with Belova, who was paying her respects there, making her presence known by blowing into a handkerchief and joking that she was "allergic to the Midwest." [[File:Valentina Allegra de Fontaine (Black Widow).png|{{AltFloat}}|De Fontaine assigns [[Yelena Belova]] a [[Hawkeye|target]]]] Belova called de Fontaine desperate for coming to her while she was on holiday and asked for a raise. De Fontaine joked that she wanted one too, before telling Belova that she had a new assignment for her. Handing her a tablet, de Fontaine told Belova that her target was Barton and claimed that he was the one responsible for Romanoff's death.{{Ref|BW}} ===Director of the CIA=== ====Anti-Wakandan Sentiments==== [[File:Valentina Allegra de Fontaine (BPWF).png|{{AltFloat}}|De Fontaine talking with the special agent]] {{Quote|It's an eight hour drive."
"Yeah, we can talk about the case. Actually, wouldn't mind apologizing to you for a couple of things I said during our marriage.|Valentina Allegra de Fontaine to [[Everett Ross]]|Black Panther: Wakanda Forever}} In [[2025|May 2025]], hearing of an event regarding [[Wakanda]]ns and the [[FBI]], de Fontaine went to [[Cambridge]], [[Massachusetts]] and requested help from [[Everett Ross]]. Upon arriving, she observed a pair of [[Kimoyo Beads]] on the ground and secretly rigged it so she could know who was using it. When Ross arrived, she complimented him on his fitness and proceeded to speak with the FBI agents. [[File:Valentina Allegra de Fontaine & Everett Ross.png|{{AltFloat}}|De Fontaine asks [[Everett Ross]] to drive her]] The Special Agent in Charge approached de Fontaine, who was rude to him. He then said he believed it was a car crash, but could not find the cars. The SAC said that the cars must have been taken and dropped into the river, which de Fontaine believed was outrageous. Afterwards, she asked Ross if he could give her a ride back to [[George Bush Center for Intelligence]] as she wanted to speak about their marriage.{{Ref|BPWF}} ====Meeting in Langley==== [[File:Valentina & Everett.png|{{AltFloat}}|De Fontaine overhearing [[Everett Ross]]' "call"]] {{Quote|I don't see how [[Wakanda|they]] figured out [[Ironheart|who]] built [[Vibranium Detector|it]]. That information is highly classified.|Valentina Allegra de Fontaine to [[Everett Ross]]|Black Panther: Wakanda Forever}} De Fontaine and [[Everett Ross]] then arrived in [[Langley]], and de Fontaine expressed her confusion as to how the [[Wakanda|Wakandans]] were able to deduce [[Ironheart|who]] built the [[Vibranium Detector]], as the information was highly classified. De Fontaine then heard a [[Kimoyo Beads|ringing]] from Ross' pocket. Addressing her as director, Ross told her he would have to answer it. De Fontaine told him he could answer it without the formalities. De Fontaine expressed surprise when she overheard Ross address his [[Ramonda|caller]] as his partner, and then left his car to enter the [[CIA]] headquarters. [[File:Valentina Allegra de Fontaine and Everett Ross.png|{{AltFloat}}|De Fontaine and [[Everett Ross]] at a meeting]] De Fontaine and Everett Ross talked with the [[U.S. State Secretary]]. The secretary brought up that the [[James Ritson|President]] wished for the CIA to commence destabilization efforts on [[Wakanda]]. De Fontaine asked Ross what he thought. Ross claimed that his sources told him there was [[Namor|another enemy]] at play. Ross asked for time to investigate, or they could find themselves at war. The secretary asked de Fontaine for her opinion, and she simply said Ross was the expert. Ross repeated himself that it would be a mistake to attack Wakanda.{{Ref|BPWF}} ====[[Arrest of Everett Ross|Confronting Her Ex-Husband]]==== [[File:Valentina Allegra de Fontaine (Black Panther Wakanda Forever).png|{{AltFloat}}|De Fontaine asks [[Everett Ross]] about lying]] {{Quote|You ever thought for a second what they could be doing? Ever thought what we would be doing if the [[United States of America|U.S.]] was the only country in the world with [[vibranium]]?"
"Oh, I actually dream about that.|[[Everett Ross]] and Valentina Allegra de Fontaine|Black Panther: Wakanda Forever}} At [[Everett Ross' House]], de Fontaine greeted Ross and showed him a news report about the death of [[Ramonda]]. As Ross expressed his shock at this news, de Fontaine pondered what other secrets the Wakandans kept from him. He attempted to bluff to her he had no unsanctioned contact with [[Wakanda]], but de Fontaine revealed she had Shuri's [[Kimoyo Beads]] bugged before he arrived at the crime scene and knew about his contact with Ramonda. [[File:Valentina with handcuffs.png|{{AltFloat}}|De Fontaine decides to arrest [[Everett Ross]]]] Ross defended his decision, stating that Wakandans are good people, before asking what the [[United States of America]] would do if they were the only ones with had access to [[vibranium]]. de Fontaine informed him that was exactly what she wanted, in fact, she dreamed of it. Ross tried to reason with de Fontaine, but she coldly refused to listen and placed him under arrest for his act of treason against the [[United States]].{{Ref|BPWF}} ===[[Prosecution of Valentina Allegra de Fontaine|Facing Impeachment]]=== ====Called to the Capitol==== {{Expand}} ''To be added''{{Ref|TB}} ====Fundraiser Party==== ''To be added''{{Ref|TB}} ====Standoff at OXE Vault==== ''To be added''{{Ref|TB}} ====Training the Sentry==== ''To be added''{{Ref|TB}} ====[[Battle at the Watchtower]]==== ''To be added''{{Ref|TB}} ====[[Attack on Manhattan]]==== ''To be added''{{Ref|TB}} ====Announcing the New Avengers==== {{Rewrite|"After" and more centralized PoV}} After [[Void]]'s actions were undone by the [[Thunderbolts]], their attention shifted towards Valentina. She initially acted scared and she tried to run away behind some debris, but it was actually all a ruse by her. In order to save face, she decided to trick the Thunderbolts into the same press conference she had prepared for [[Sentry]], to announce them as the [[New Avengers]], taking all the credit. While this worked, it also meant her ex-agents now owned her. In the following months, Valentina provided the New Avengers with a rebranded [[Watchtower]], alongside new equipment and even their own merchandise.{{Ref|TB}} ==Abilities== *'''Master Tactician''': De Fontaine is very skilled at forming teams and manipulating people. She was able to recruit both [[John Walker]] and [[Yelena Belova]]. When [[Eleanor Bishop]] called her to get rid of [[Clint Barton]], de Fontaine manipulated Belova into thinking that Barton killed her sister, [[Natasha Romanoff]] so that Belova would finish the mission with extreme lethality. Additionally, she chose her own ambitions over her ex-husband and bugged [[Kimoyo Beads]] to prove he was committing treason. *'''Bilingualism''': De Fontaine is fluent in her native Italian and English. *'''Expert Combatant''': De Fontaine is capable of subduing individuals who are larger than her. ==Facilities== *'''[[Everett Ross' House]]''': De Fontaine used to live at the house when she was married to [[Everett Ross]]. She later visited the house to arrest Ross. *'''[[United States Capitol]]''': De Fontaine met up with [[Olivia Walker]] and her husband [[John Walker]] at the United States Capitol following the latter's status as Captain America being stripped away. *'''[[George Bush Center for Intelligence]]''': As director of the [[CIA]], de Fontaine worked at the George Bush Center for Intelligence. She attended a meeting there with [[Everett Ross]] regarding political tensions rising between the [[United States of America]] and [[Wakanda]]. *'''[[Watchtower]]''': Valentina was the one who bought [[Avengers Tower]], renovating it (despite not continuously) into the Watchtower for her [[Project Sentry]] eventual results. ==Relationships== {{Col-begin}}{{Col-2}} ===Family=== *Father † *[[Everett Ross]] - Ex-Husband and Former Subordinate ===Allies=== *[[O.X.E.]] - Subordinates **[[Mel Gold]] **[[Holt (O.X.E.)|Holt]] **[[Lima]] **[[Foxtrot (O.X.E.)|Foxtrot]] **[[Tango (O.X.E.)|Tango]] *[[CIA]] - Subordinates *[[Olivia Walker]] *[[Eleanor Bishop]] - Client *[[FBI]] *[[United States Department of State]] **[[U.S. State Secretary]] *[[United States Department of Defense]] **[[Anderson (DOD)|Anderson]] {{Col-2}} ===Enemies=== *[[Nico (Italy)|Nico]] *[[Hawkeye|Clint Barton/Hawkeye]] *[[Project Sentry]] - Former Subordinates **[[Houston]] † *[[Taskmaster|Antonia Dreykov/Taskmaster]] † - Former Recruit *[[United States Congress]] **[[Gary (Congressman)|Gary]] **[[Davis (Congresswoman)|Davis]] **[[Carter (Congressman)|Carter]] **[[Brown (Congressman)|Brown]] **[[Elkins]] *[[New Avengers]] - Situational Allies and Attempted Victims **[[Yelena Belova]] - Former Recruit **[[U.S. Agent|John Walker/Captain America/U.S. Agent]] - Former Recruit **[[Ghost|Ava Starr/Ghost]] - Former Recruit **[[Winter Soldier|Bucky Barnes/Winter Soldier]] **[[Red Guardian|Alexei Shostakov/Red Guardian]] *[[Sentry|Bob Reynolds/Sentry/Void]] - Former Test Subject and Attempted Killer {{Col-end}} ==Appearances== In chronological order: *{{Appearance|TB}} (flashbacks) *{{Appearance|TFaTWS105}} *{{Appearance|TFaTWS106}} *{{Appearance|BW}} *{{Appearance|BPWF}} *{{Appearance|TBD}} *{{Appearance|TB}} ==Trivia== *In the comics, Contessa '''Valentina Allegra de la Fontaine''' is an alleged Italian noblewoman who joined [[S.H.I.E.L.D.]] and even ascended temporarily to the rank of Deputy Director, becoming [[Nick Fury]]'s lover. Eventually, she infiltrated [[Hydra]] and assumed the identity of '''Madame Hydra''', though she was actually a Russian triple agent, having worked for [[Leviathan]] as a sleeper agent since before being recruited by S.H.I.E.L.D. **In the [[Marvel Cinematic Universe]], she shares similarities and covers a similar role to '''Valerie "Val" Cooper''', a government advisor and liaison specialized in regulations of superhuman activities in the [[United States of America]] who recruited and collaborated with [[John Walker]] for many years. *De Fontaine is allergic to pollen. ==Behind the Scenes== *Valentina Allegra de Fontaine in the [[Marvel Cinematic Universe]] is the second live-action incarnation of the character, following an incarnation portrayed by {{WPS|Lisa Rinna}}. *[[Julia Louis-Dreyfus]] was set to be introduced as Valentina Allegra de Fontaine in ''[[Black Widow (film)|Black Widow]]'' before her appearance in ''[[The Falcon and The Winter Soldier]]'', but this was changed due to delays caused by the {{WPS|COVID-19 pandemic}}.[https://www.vanityfair.com/hollywood/2021/04/julia-louis-dreyfus-marvel-villain-character-contessa-valentina-allegra-de-fontaine Marvel Has Big Plans for That Surprise ''The Falcon and The Winter Soldier'' Cameo] *In an early draft for ''[[Black Panther: Wakanda Forever]]'', when de Fontaine [[Arrest of Everett Ross|arrested]] [[Everett Ross]], she revealed that she had a recording of [[Graham (Doctor)|Graham]]'s final phone call about the [[Talokanil]] but kept it to herself. ==References== {{Gallery|Valentina Allegra de Fontaine/Gallery}} {{Reflist}} ==External Links== *{{Marvel}} *{{Marvel|Valerie Cooper}} *{{WP}} *{{WP|Valerie Cooper}} [[es:Valentina Allegra de Fontaine]] [[fr:Valentina Allegra de Fontaine]]
